name: How do I prove my hearing loss is from the factory and not just getting older?

mainEntity.name: How do I prove my hearing loss is from the factory and not just getting older?

acceptedAnswer.text: Success requires an audiogram showing specific noise-induced patterns and a medical opinion linking that damage to workplace decibel levels. Comparing your hearing to people of the same age who don't work in factories is a common strategy to prove causation.
acceptedAnswer.description: Causation is the "battleground" of every occupational disease case. To prove it, we gather employment records showing your job duties, air quality or noise level samples, and testimonies from coworkers. Your medical records must explicitly link the diagnosis to the workplace. For example, if you have carpal tunnel, your doctor must state that the repetition at work was the primary cause, not your cell phone use or guitar playing. In Utah, we often use the "Allen Test" or similar legal frameworks to prove the workplace increased your risk beyond what you'd face in everyday life. If the insurer denies you based on "age," we bring in medical specialists to refute them.
